text:
*[Bug 19010] Compilation fails with Intel OneAPI 2024.2 compiler*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19010>

--- Comment #2 from Martin Maechler (<mailto:maechler@stat.math.ethz.ch|maechler@stat.math.ethz.ch>) ---
Actually, I've opened the URL you provided,  
   <https://github.com/r-hub/containers/actions/runs/22427885178/job/64940233556>
looking into the 4th item, `Build R` and its log, showing `configure` outputs, 
(preceeded by `intel-1  | ` ) where indeed,
on line 299 it shows 
>    checking for atan2pi... yes

but as we see later, the compiler can *not* find `atan2pi()`

To me this indicates that your local / platform specific configuration, must be
wrong: the above 'Build R' log, lines 13 and 14 are
```
intel-1  | loading site script './config.site'
intel-1  | loading build-specific script './config.site'
```

which indicates you are using even **two** `<http://config.site|config.site>` configuration tweaking
files.  They must some lead to this fact that the intel compiler when compiling
`plot3d.c` does not use the correct `-I<...>`  (even though it uses quite a
few)
and even _different_  `-I<....>` include paths than during configuration where
it was used and did find `atan2pi()` otherwise we'd not see the `checking for
atan2pi... yes` mentioned above.

Even if this may be reproducible via the docker image, I don't think it should
be our problem to debug why your (or possibly Intel's oneAPI) compiler setup
leads to incompatible configuration vs later compilation.

I'd tend close this as something that needs to be fixed on that platform.

text:
*[Bug 19011] Fix for cdplot returning probabilities greater than One*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19011>

--- Comment #1 from Christoph Dalitz (<mailto:christoph.dalitz@hs-niederrhein.de|christoph.dalitz@hs-niederrhein.de>) ---
Created attachment 3618
  --> <https://bugs.r-project.org/attachment.cgi?id=3618&action=edit>
Corrected version of replacement file for cdplot.R

Just noticed that, in the computation of the a priori probabilities `py` in the
computation of the undonditional density by means of the law of total
probability, the weights were not used.

The line

    py <- proportions(table(y))

in the previous fix should thus be changed to

    if(is.null(weights)) {
      py <- proportions(table(y))
    } else {
      py <- proportions(tapply(weights, y, FUN = sum, na.rm = TRUE))
      py[<http://is.na|is.na>(py)] <- 0
    }


For convenience, I have attached the complete changed file that should replace
`cdplot.R`.

Sorry for the inconveneince,

Christoph Dalitz

text:
*[Bug 18989] Wishlist: provide conversion of POSIXlt time zone retaining sub-second accuracy*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=18989>

--- Comment #8 from Suharto Anggono (<mailto:suharto_anggono@yahoo.com|suharto_anggono@yahoo.com>) ---
(In reply to Suharto Anggono from comment #1)
From my testing with distant times, the above is not so sound. When the
precision of POSIXct is too poor to represent the fractional seconds, it may be
rounded up.

x <- as.POSIXlt("9999-12-31 23:59:59.99999", tz="UTC")
print.default(as.POSIXlt(as.POSIXct(x)))

gives


$sec
[1] 0

$min
[1] 0

$hour
[1] 0

$mday
[1] 1

$mon
[1] 0

$year
[1] 8100

$wday
[1] 6

$yday
[1] 0

$isdst
[1] 0

$zone
[1] "UTC"

$gmtoff
[1] 0

attr(,"class")
[1] "POSIXlt" "POSIXt" 
attr(,"tzone")
[1] "UTC"
attr(,"balanced")
[1] TRUE

text:
*[Bug 18989] Wishlist: provide conversion of POSIXlt time zone retaining sub-second accuracy*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=18989>

--- Comment #9 from Suharto Anggono (<mailto:suharto_anggono@yahoo.com|suharto_anggono@yahoo.com>) ---
(In reply to Suharto Anggono from comment #8)
It is better to take floor of 'sec' component before converting to POSIXct.

In 'POSIXltconv',
    s <- s - floor(s)
can be moved to right after
    stopifnot(is.numeric(s <- x$sec))
and become
    s <- s - (x$sec <- floor(s))
.

In 'c.POSIXlt',
    n <- lengths(x <- lapply(x, as.POSIXct), use.names = FALSE)
can be replaced with
    x <- lapply(x, function(x) {
        if(inherits(x, "POSIXlt")) x$sec <- floor(x$sec)
        as.POSIXct(x)
    })
    n <- lengths(x, use.names = FALSE)
.

Also,
        x$sec[i] <- floor(x$sec[i]) + s[i]
becomes
        x$sec[i] <- x$sec[i] + s[i]
.

However, for more distant times, maybe in range where not all whole numbers can
be represented by 'double' type, it can still be wrong.

x <- as.POSIXlt("9999-12-31 23:59:59", tz="UTC")
x$year <- 3e8 - 1
print(2^53, 17)
print.default(as.POSIXct(x), 17)
print.default(as.POSIXlt(as.POSIXct(x)))

gives

[1] 9007199254740992
[1] 9467083391011200
attr(,"class")
[1] "POSIXct" "POSIXt" 
attr(,"tzone")
[1] "UTC"
$sec
[1] 0

$min
[1] 0

$hour
[1] 0

$mday
[1] 1

$mon
[1] 0

$year
[1] 300000000

$wday
[1] 1

$yday
[1] 0

$isdst
[1] 0

$zone
[1] "UTC"

$gmtoff
[1] 0

attr(,"class")
[1] "POSIXlt" "POSIXt" 
attr(,"tzone")
[1] "UTC"
attr(,"balanced")
[1] TRUE

text:
*[Bug 19014] New: is.character is not in the S3 generic list (and a few others, too)*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19014>

            Bug ID: 19014
           Summary: is.character is not in the S3 generic list (and a few
                    others, too)
           Product: R
           Version: R 4.5.x
          Hardware: All
                OS: All
            Status: UNCONFIRMED
          Severity: minor
          Priority: P5
         Component: Low-level
          Assignee: <mailto:R-core@R-project.org|R-core@R-project.org>
          Reporter: <mailto:bill.r-project@denney.ws|bill.r-project@denney.ws>

As initially noted here (<https://github.com/r-lib/roxygen2/issues/1717>),
`is.character()` does not show up as an S3 generic method. Some others like
is.list() and is.environment() also do not show up there. It would be useful to
have an audit of all the is.* functions; I don't know why some may show up in
those lists or not.

Based on the roxygen2 bug, I think that these should be added so that they show
up in either `.knownS3Generics` or `tools:::.get_internal_S3_generics()`.

--- Comment #3 from Hadley Wickham (<mailto:h.wickham@gmail.com|h.wickham@gmail.com>) ---
I think this is a valid bug — `is.character()` is an S3 generic but is not
listed along with other primitive generics. I don't think there are any other
functions in this category; most of the other `is.` functions are not generics.

text:
*[Bug 19014] is.character is not in the S3 generic list (and a few others, too)*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19014>

Simon Urbanek (<mailto:simon.urbanek@r-project.org|simon.urbanek@r-project.org>) changed:

           What    |Removed                     |Added
----------------------------------------------------------------------------
                 CC|                            |simon.urbanek@r-project.org

--- Comment #8 from Simon Urbanek (<mailto:simon.urbanek@r-project.org|simon.urbanek@r-project.org>) ---
Hold on, I think you misinterpreted Luke's response. I don't think there is an
issue, but it requires reading the documentation more closely: `as.character`
is generic, i.e. you can write methods for it, but it is NOT a registered S3
generic since it is a primitive - that's why the documentation points to
`?InternalMethods` which points to `.S3PrimitiveGenerics` where it is correctly
listed, and why it cannot be in `.knownS3Generics` as those are mutually
exclusive.

We are discussing `is.character`, not `as.character`.  My comment 7 is
proposing more or less just the following:

```
Index: src/library/base/man/InternalMethods.Rd

+++ src/library/base/man/InternalMethods.Rd     (working copy)
@@ -117,8 +117,7 @@

   \code{\link{xtfrm}}%                               [sort.c]

-  In addition, \code{is.name} is a synonym for \code{\link{is.symbol}} and
-  dispatches methods for the latter.  Similarly, \code{\link{as.numeric}}
+  \code{\link{as.numeric}}
   is a synonym for \code{as.double} and dispatches methods for the
   latter, i.e., S3 methods are for \code{as.double}, whereas S4 methods
   are to be written for \code{as.numeric}.

===================================================================

+++ src/library/base/man/character.Rd   (working copy)
@@ -26,7 +26,7 @@
   \item{\dots}{further arguments passed to or from other methods.}
 }
 \details{
-  \code{as.character} and \code{is.character} are generic: you can
+  \code{as.character} is generic: you can
   write methods to handle specific classes of objects,
   see \link{InternalMethods}.  Further, for \code{as.character} the
   default method calls \code{\link{as.vector}}, so, only
```

text:
*[Bug 19014] is.character is not in the S3 generic list (and a few others, too)*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19014>

Sebastian Meyer (<mailto:seb.meyer@fau.de|seb.meyer@fau.de>) changed:

           What    |Removed                     |Added
----------------------------------------------------------------------------
                 CC|                            |seb.meyer@fau.de

--- Comment #10 from Sebastian Meyer (<mailto:seb.meyer@fau.de|seb.meyer@fau.de>) ---
Yes, these documentation pieces were outdated since a change in R 2.5.0:

    o   is.atomic, is.call, is.character, [...]
        and is.symbol (== is.name) are no
        longer internally S3 generic, nor can S4 methods be written
        for them.

Now updated in r89535 as suggested.
